programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Trischa Price investigates a report of a sick horse, but is confused when she finds discarded hypodermic needles nearby

programme.title: Humphrey Burton: The Huw Wheldon Lecture
programme.desc: The 1991 address by the producer and director in which he talks about the achievements of Monitor, the BBCs first TV arts magazine, and reflects on what programme-makers have to learn from those pioneering days. Among the examples used by Burton are films commissioned by Huw Wheldon from Ken Russell and John Schlesinger, two young directors who went on to greater things, and studio interviews with Orson Welles and Ninette de Valois

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Janet Street-Porter visits some of the architectural highlights of North Yorkshire, including Scarborough, Castle Howard, Ebberston Hall, Mount Grace Priory and Middlesbroughs Transporter Bridge

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planets most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The cultural historian heads to the vast Konya Plain of Turkey, tracking a young explorer who found the worlds oldest city and rewrote the beginning of civilisation - until his reputation was destroyed by scandal

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson presides over another duel of words and wits as Frank Muir, Prunella Gee and Humphrey Burton take on Patrick Campbell, Hannah Gordon and Bryan Marshall. First broadcast in 1977

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Brian Redhead and Alvar Lidell. With guest musician David Atherton. First broadcast in 1975

programme.title: The Secret Rules of Modern Living: Algorithms
programme.desc: Without people noticing, modern life has been taken over. Algorithms run everything, from search engines on the internet, to satnavs and credit-card security - they even help people travel the world, find love and save lives. Here, mathematician Marcus du Sautoy demystifies this hidden world by showing some of the most essential algorithms. He reveals where these 2,000-year-old problem-solvers came from, showing how they work, what they have achieved and how they are now so advanced they can even program themselves

programme.title: Bridget Riley: Painting the Line
programme.desc: Bridget Riley has been challenging perception through painting for more than 60 years, with radical work that has transformed how many look at art. This documentary, featuring behind-the-scenes access and an interview with Kirsty Wark, tells the story of a true visionary of British art

programme.title: Nature and Us: A History Through Art
programme.desc: James Fox tells the story of the human races ever-changing relationship with nature through the lens of some of the worlds most extraordinary artworks, beginning with a look back at the art of the ancient world, looking at the art of prehistoric hunters, the advent of agriculture, the first cities, and the beginnings of Hinduism and Christianity

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planets most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Brian Redhead and Alvar Lidell. With guest musician David Atherton. First broadcast in 1975

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The cultural historian heads to the vast Konya Plain of Turkey, tracking a young explorer who found the worlds oldest city and rewrote the beginning of civilisation - until his reputation was destroyed by scandal

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson reveals how some of the nations best-loved villages played a vital part in significant moments of history. He begins in Bamburgh in Northumberland, explaining how the health and fortunes of the village were shaped by those in charge of its famous castle, from early English kings to 18th-century philanthropists, who pioneered a welfare system nearly 200 years before one existed nationally. Ben visits fellow archaeologist Jessica Turner, whose findings show the seventh-century residents of Bamburgh were robust and healthy - contrary to the usual malnourishment of the British medieval population

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on a two-day walk along the Worcester and Birmingham Canal, which cuts a 30-mile path to the River Severn, discovering how it helped to generate wealth in the Industrial Revolution. Along the way, she follows a two-mile flight of 30 locks that lower the canal 220ft

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth feels harassed by a combination of working at a charity shop, trying to impress Councillor Nugent and worrying about Roses love life. To cap it all, Daisy informs her that a strange woman in Daddys bedroom claims to be his fiancee. Starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy takes his mothers advice when an insanely jealous man takes an interest in his girlfriend. Ronnie Corbett stars

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: The story of the Battle of Hastings in 1066, which saw King Harold beaten after leading an army worn out from fighting his brother Tostig and a horde of Vikings, to confront William the Conqueror. The Saxon defeat led to 300 years of Norman hegemony and a country plagued by massacre and famine, although the new rulers managed to introduce and keep an inventory of all land and possessions, better known as the Domesday Book

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A look at the emergence of a discreet new industry in contemporary China, preserving marriages threatened by infidelity. The film follows the emotional fault lines of a real love triangle as it unfolds, capturing a woman hiring a professional mistress dispeller to infiltrate her husbands affair and dismantle it from within

programme.title: Border Country: The Story of Britains Lost Middleland
programme.desc: Part two of two. Rory Stewart reveals how a thousand years after the Romans left, Britain split once again near the line of Hadrians Wall into the kingdoms of England and Scotland. The historian examines how the borderlands became a zone of anarchy and violence

programme.title: Bridget Riley: Painting the Line
programme.desc: Bridget Riley has been challenging perception through painting for more than 60 years, with radical work that has transformed how many look at art. This documentary, featuring behind-the-scenes access and an interview with Kirsty Wark, tells the story of a true visionary of British art

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make the day that little bit easier

programme.title: Crimewatch Live
programme.desc: Appeals for information on the murder of Diane Sindall, who was found dead in an alleyway in 1986. Plus, how officers rescued a woman trapped on Aberavon Beach, Port Talbot

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is served up a treat at a Wimbledon flat and Martel Maxwell likes the character at a Sunderland property. Dion Dublin is in Doncaster, where he dishes out a yellow card to a developer who hasnt viewed his new auction purchase

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents as experts Izzie Balmer and Nick Hall shop alongside the red and blue teams at Bingley Hall, Staffordshire

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: The presenter tries to resolve conflicts in the wilderness, uniting people with unresolved feuds for a bespoke challenge set by a team of experts that will force them to work together. Former best friends Rhys and Niall work to repair their broken friendship. They drifted apart after the death of Nialls older brother and have barely spoken for two years

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr embark on their first ever Spanish renovation job. Hoping to breathe new life into one of Andalusias forgotten villages, Amanda takes a gamble on a run-down three-storey townhouse and convinces Alan to help her to transform it into a boutique BandB. In a bid to push their DIY skills to a whole new level, the pair visit Granadas architectural marvel and the pinnacle of Moorish design - the Alhambra Palace. However, with over 2,000 square feet to contend with, a roof in need of repair and a crumbling outbuilding on its last legs, it seems they may be in over their heads

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In Scotlands Central Lowlands, Sonali Shah helps a Canadian couple to make a rural escape and meets Californians who bought their dream home, sight unseen, and never looked back

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk travel around the UK fixing historical treasures and precious objects that are unable be brought to the programmes barn. In Chatham, they restore two vandalised memorial plaques for the fallen of the First World War on behalf of a proud military community, with the aid of a local bronze foundry

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Louise, Andrew, Warren, Grace and Jourdan race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Ed Gamble for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Under pressure from the gang to get rid of the informant, Mark searches for Ravi before finding him at the Boxing Den. Lauren worries about her future, as news reaches her that the car lot is up for sale, and Phil avoids a situation

programme.title: Jane Reas Bloomin Wild
programme.desc: It is winter in Shilnavogie, and the daily routine of feeding animals and keeping the farm tidy is easier said than done now that Jane is seven months pregnant. In the polytunnel, Jane is already thinking ahead to next year, where her visit to Glenarm Castle Walled Garden in the spring has inspired her to start a tulip patch of her own. She is also on a mission to save her dwindling flock of Jacob sheep

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: At War: Trump, Israel and Iran - Panorama
programme.desc: Jane Corbin investigates the origins and aims of the US/Israeli offensive against Iran, and the future of the relationship between the UK and the US in light of Keir Starmers refusal to allow the use of UK bases for the first attacks. The programme asks what the campaign is achieving, how far it could escalate, and what it means for ordinary people caught in the fighting

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Mervin stumbles across a crime scene when he goes in search of his brother Solomon on Antigua. In a remote cabin, a local businessman has been shot. But Solomon and his associate Cheech both swear the other is responsible, and theyre not letting Mervin go until each one has convinced him of their account

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: Trying
programme.desc: Jason resigns himself to Tyler being separated from his sister, so he decides to give the kids a memorable time. Vic, however, is not one to give up without a fight

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a one-bedroom flat on the Jurassic Coast in Devon, whose new owner is really getting their teeth into the interior design. Jacqui Joseph doesnt know if shes looking around two flats or one house in east London, and Dion Dublin is in Yorkshire, where work is already under way at a three-bedroom semi

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

Rachel is desperate to build a loving and respectful relationship between them - one she feels she missed out on with her own mother. As they embark on a kayaking task, it isnt long before emotions run high and tempers flare

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr set about designing a Spanish dining room and foyer. Alans dreams of a lavish reception area are soon quashed when Amanda suggests his special space should be a cupboard under the stairs. To get some impressive design inspiration, the duo head to the outskirts of Vejer to a boutique hotel filled with local salvaged treasures. Amanda also visits the largest reclamation yard in Andalusia to upcycle an old Spanish door into a statement dining table, but Alan fears she may have taken her upcycling too far and is bluntly honest with his friend

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson meets retiree Jenny who is moving from Kent to the North Wessex Downs to be closer to her son, Ollie, and is hoping her £300,000 budget will find her a rosy cottage to call home

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: In Birmingham, Will Kirk and Dominic Chinea turn a neglected phone box into a life-saving defibrillator station, while in west London, Will learns the historical and romantic art of fan-making with expert Victoria Ajoko. Back at the barn, Dom uncovers a pair of secateurs owned by a D-Day paratrooper

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz, which sees contestants Ben, Habiba, Howie, Mia and Dave race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Priya panics about Ravis safety, and heads to find Vicki to reveal all. Following their respective conversations with Peter and Jack, its clear that Max and Lauren both have very different ideas on how their business partnerships is going to run

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and her team of experts are back to help more families declutter their homes. They begin with the Kings in Fife - retired architectural technician Gerry, care worker Trish and two of their three grown-up children. Their house contains 30 years of memories, knick-knacks and general clutter - not to mention countless unfinished DIY projects. During the process, the team learn Gerry has recently been diagnosed with early onset dementia, so it is important they hold on to items that will continue to spark memories for him while losing any clutter so it is easy for him to move around

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The best chefs from the heats return for knockout week, and are given the run of the larder as they are challenged to deliver an impressive dish. The strongest eight sail through to the semi-finals, while the rest cook again, using whatever leftovers remain in stock - and in a twist, if they served a savoury dish last time, they must now come up with a dessert or vice versa. Monica Galetti, Marcus Wareing and Matt Tebbutt judge their efforts

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Marie-Louise Connolly investigates the difficult choice facing new mothers who experience serious mental health issues after giving birth. In Northern Ireland, treatment means being separated from their babies. This is the only part of the UK without a specialist mother and baby unit that would that allow in-patient treatment with their baby beside them

programme.title: Marry Me (2022)
programme.desc: Music superstars Kat and Bastian are preparing to get married before a global audience of fans. But when Kat learns, seconds before her vows, that Bastian has been unfaithful, she instead decides to marry Charlie, a stranger in the crowd. Romantic comedy, starring Jennifer Lopez, Owen Wilson and Maluma

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts as the satirical current affairs quiz returns for its 69th run. Team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton are joined by broadcaster Steph McGovern and comedian Phil Wang as guest panellists

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara McDermott meets men chasing cheap thrills in Asias sex capital, Pattaya, and also talks to a local sex worker, who reveals the harsh reality of working in the illegal sex industry. She then heads to Ko Phangan, an exotic island famed for its spirituality and beauty. For Zara, this is paradise - but Lucio, a Brit who built a burgeoning cannabis business here while the drug was decriminalised, reveals that navigating the local law isnt always easy

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara McDermott experiences Ko Phangans famous Full Moon Party, but beneath the cheap cocktails, music and fire shows, theres danger lurking as the medical team treat a possible drink-spiking victim. She then travels to Phuket and experiences a taste of Thailands luxury lifestyle. But she also learns how dream holidays can quickly go wrong, hearing the case of a British holiday-maker who died on the island - and whose family is still searching for answers

programme.title: Missing in Paradise: Searching for Sarm
programme.desc: Journalist Tir Dhondy investigates the disappearance of Sarm Heslop, who went missing from a luxury Caribbean charter yacht in 2021. Tire retraces Sarms last known movements and tracks down two boat captains whose vessels were moored near the yacht she vanished from. Also, she gets to question Ryan Banes, Sarms boyfriend, account of what happened what happened through his lawyer David Cattie

programme.title: Missing in Paradise: Searching for Sarm
programme.desc: Tir Dhondy meets with island police and is given CCTV footage that has never been released, showing Sarms last known movements with her boyfriend, Ryan Bane

programme.title: Missing in Paradise: Searching for Sarm
programme.desc: Tir Dhondy searches for the yacht Sarm vanished from, and tracks down Ryan Banes ex-wife, hearing first-hand about her experiences while married to him

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Its Tommys birthday, but theres no time for cake. With just days to go before his long-awaited comeback fight in Budapest, Tommy is pulled in every direction - juggling press conferences, promo shoots and the pressure of proving hes still got what it takes in the ring

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Waterloo Road
programme.desc: First episode of the drama set in a comprehensive school in Rochdale, Greater Manchester. Acting headteacher Jack Rimmer hires Oxbridge graduate Andrew Treneman, hoping he can improve their failing status and bring them back from the bottom of the league tables. Colleagues Tom and Lorna tie the knot - but the groom ends up kissing someone who isnt his new wife. Jason Merrells, Jill Halfpenny, Angela Griffin and Jamie Glover star

programme.title: Waterloo Road
programme.desc: Donte and Chlo are plagued with guilt over their involvement in a fatal car crash, and suffer malicious taunts from all corners of the playground. Izzie feels awkward after kissing Tom at his wedding reception, while he remains unsure about exactly what he wants. Andrew comes into conflict with Kim, but they soon begin to see eye to eye

programme.title: Blade Runner 2049 (2017)

Denis Villeneuves sci-fi thriller, the sequel to Ridley Scotts 1982 classic, starring Ryan Gosling, Harrison Ford, Ana de Armas and Sylvia Hoeks

programme.title: Men of the Manosphere
programme.desc: James Blake enters the virtual world of manhood, often referred to as the manosphere, in search of status, wealth and relationships. He meets three young men who have turned to male social media influencers for guidance and instruction as they seek to better themselves and achieve success in different areas of their lives, both on and offline

programme.title: EastEnders Investigates: The Manosphere
programme.desc: An investigation into the issues of misogyny and toxic masculinity, to coincide with the current EastEnders storyline centred on teenager Joel Marshall, played by Max Murray

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Its Tommys birthday, but theres no time for cake. With just days to go before his long-awaited comeback fight in Budapest, Tommy is pulled in every direction - juggling press conferences, promo shoots and the pressure of proving hes still got what it takes in the ring

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: After the success of his comeback fight, Tommys road to redemption hits a major setback - his hand is injured again and needs eight weeks total rest before doctors will know if he can fight again. The last time he couldnt train, his life spiralled out of control, so now Tommy throws himself into a frenzy of activity - a TV appearance on The Wheel, family days out with Bambi and a final push to finish the home he hopes Molly and Bambi will soon return to

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommys recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommys next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mother out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy realises just how far he and Molly have come since the separation, but Johns worries grow as Tommy takes on a 100km triathlon, pushing his body to new physical limits. Before the challenge, Tommy takes some downtime at the spa with his mates, preparing for a huge press push ahead of his series premiere. After completing a series of intense physical challenges, he has to deal with a social media backlash

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan asks general knowledge questions against the clock as celebrity contestants try to avoid being voted off the team. This weeks contestants are Marcus Brigstocke, Rita Simons, Jamie Laing, Nicki Chapman, Babatunde Aleshe, Yvette Fielding, Tony Blackburn and Chunkz

programme.title: Alan Carrs Picture Slam
programme.desc: Alan Carr hosts the game show in which three pairs of contestants try to identify a board full of images, with a chance of winning £10,000 if they know them all. Hoping to take home the jackpot this week are Essex married couple Nic and Corin, father-son duo Alex and David from Aberdeenshire, and Glasgow and Durham friends Abbie and Maddy

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: This week, the makeover challenge goes Olympic as the queens drag up a host of Team GB Olympians, including Greg Rutherford, Shanaze Reade, Molly Thompson-Smith, Celia Quansah and Daniel Jervis. Pop superstar Anastacia joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Graham Norton

programme.title: Cat Person (2023)
programme.desc: A student begins dating an older man and realises that his persona in real-life doesnt match his flirty texts. Tense drama, starring Emilia Jones, Nicholas Braun and Geraldine Viswanathan

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: The story of Kilmarnock nurse Adele Rennie, who pretended to be handsome, professional men to target, harass, stalk and exploit hundreds of women. When Dr David Graham starts flirting with former beauty queen Abbie Draper online, she turns sleuth and uncovers a trail of deception, crazy coincidences and stories that just dont add up

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: Abbie Draper takes her findings to the police, but they are unable to do anything about it and the Catfish of Kilmarnock remains free to target more and more victims. However, Abbie cannot walk away and steps up her investigation over months that turn into years, with victims spread across the country and dating back over a decade

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: The victims finally have justice, but are still left urgently searching for answers. What drives someone, with a seemingly normal life to terrorise countless women from the same town? Those closest to the perpetrator, who are also trying to understand the mystery behind the repeated cycles of offending, share their thoughts

He meets three young men who have turned to male social media influencers for guidance and instruction as they seek to better themselves and achieve success in different areas of their lives, both on and offline

Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair

programme.title: Weather Watchers with Barra Best
programme.desc: The weather presenter and journalist examines how climate shapes everyones lives and looks at the activities that it enables people to participate in

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nikki, Jack, Harriet and Kit must navigate complex international tensions as they investigate the apparent suicide of a British-Chinese pro-democracy activist. Nikki uncovers evidence the woman was already dead before she supposedly drowned herself, and that she may have had a price on her head set by the Hong Kong government, who viewed her as a threat to national security

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience in Kettering, Northamptonshire

programme.desc: Founder of Tropic Skincare, Susie Ma, joins Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Steven Bartlett in the den. The Dragons hear pitches for a nose-friendly home composter, a pre-loved kitchen business a new design for a medical bag and a DIY drinks infusion kit. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Highlights of the fifth-round ties, including Fulham v Southampton at Craven Cottage, and Leeds United v Norwich City at Elland Road. Top-flight sides Fulham and Leeds will both have expected to progress to the last eight at home to Championship outfits, with United boss Daniel Farke taking on his former club. Plus, a round-up of Saturdays action

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz in which one contestant is put in control and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which of the other players is best suited to answer

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads for the hills above Aberdyfi, where he explores the surrounding countryside above the Dyfi estuary and enjoys a walk along the beach

programme.title: New Zealand: Earths Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealands weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel introduces the show from the Victoria and Albert Museum, where experts value family treasures including a painting, an original handwritten poem by WB Yeats, early Meissen porcelain and a rare silver tobacco box

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Kate Bateman and Adam Partridge value items in the spa town of Malvern, Worcestershire, including a set of playing cards and a bronze statue. Presenter Paul Martin also takes time out to visit Whitley Court, the huge ruins of a former stately home

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith face off, demonstrating the general knowledge and trivia skills that made them champions last time

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Host Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton test Owain Wyn Evans, Greg Rutherford and Abby Cook on their knowledge of the UK, beginning with Belfast

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotlands Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton begins this episode at Loch Glashan above Loch Fyne, where Paul travels back in time to learn how to paddle a coracle. At Old Castle Lachlan, he dabbles in Victorian virtual reality, courtesy of George Washington Wilson and his stereoscopic camera, before visiting the open-air museum of Auchindrain

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie hosts the first semi-final, asking questions on the specialist subjects of Fleabag, Michael Faraday, the presidency of Ronald Reagan, and British marine invertebrates, before the contestants are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Amol Rajan asks the questions as the quarter-final stage continues with the University of Sheffield taking on Imperial College London. Both teams won their first match, meaning the winner of this tie goes through to the semi-finals

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: The mathematician explores the link between a controversial AI algorithm and Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. There were allegations the company used artificial intelligence to deny insurance claims from vulnerable patients, while Mangione himself studied AI at university. Hannah travels to the US to meet a woman who lost her husband and claims it happened after he was denied healthcare by an algorithm, and she also speaks to a UnitedHealthcare insider who reveals her concerns about their use of AI

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.sub-title: Pauline Scanlon, Caoimhe Hopkinson and Austin Walkin Cane
programme.desc: Singer Pauline Scanlon and guitarist and bassist Caoimhe Hopkinson are joined by American blues musician Austin Walkin Cane for a journey of musical collaboration. In Irish

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day, presented by Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Small Prophets
programme.desc: Strange things are happening in Marvin Gardens and they are starting to attract unwanted attention. Gordon threatens to fire Michael, while Brian has received a letter from an old friend, and a hole in the roof leads to a discovery. Kacey and Michael exhume the prophets from their pile of manure to see if they have reached the state of divination and the ability to predict the future. Comedy written and directed by Mackenzie Crook, starring Pearce Quigley and Lauren Patel

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Speckled Band
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret are packing for a holiday in Athens - but their preparations are delayed by an unhelpful electricity board representative, as well as an escaped snake. Starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie, with Clive Mantle

programme.title: The Last Black Man in San Francisco (2019)
programme.desc: Jimmie Fails yearns to reclaim a sense of belonging in his native San Francisco, where the rapid spread of gentrification continues to push out communities already on the margins. Accompanied by his best friend Mont, Jimmie embarks on an odyssey to recover his childhood home, a house built by his grandfather in a now-costly neighborhood of the city. Drama, starring Jimmie Fails, Jonathan Majors and Rob Morgan.

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mei Li reveals her life is at risk because she is a pro-democracy protester who organised a demonstration to free a political prisoner. Kit finds the arrest warrant for Mei Li, which states there is a bounty of $1 million for her capture, leading the team to wonder if they are looking at state-sponsored murder. However, the case takes an unexpected turn with the death of a second victim, while Burns reveals the identity of the dead woman originally thought to have been Mei Li. But as Nikki follows up further evidence, she soon finds herself in danger

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: On the Eve of the Salmon Season: The River Tweed
programme.desc: Sammi Kinghorn and Matt Baker join the start of salmon fishing season on the River Tweed, meeting the people keeping traditions alive, the scientists monitoring the rivers health, and the rod-makers and tweed weavers hard at work behind the scenes. Plus, Tom Heap investigates a surge in illegal hare coursing thats left farmers in some areas fearing for their own safety

programme.desc: Married couple Steve and Jenny are looking to buy their first home together but cant agree on what they want. Bear puts the two through a raft-building challenge to highlight how they solve problems together before they each have to take a leap of faith. Mediator Dave helps the couple work out why theyve struggled to resolve the issue so far

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: This walk takes Derek Brockway to the remote Berwyn mountains, where he walks from Llangynog to the spectacular Pistyll Rhaeadr waterfall, one of the seven wonders of Wales

programme.title: New Zealand: Earths Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealands wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel hosts as the team value items in Barnstaple, north Devon. Their visit turns up an enamel miniature by Henry Bone, a First World War pilots watch once worn by TE Lawrence, a collection of walking sticks, a painting from the Newlyn School and a native Canadian octopus bag from the 19th century

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin leads a team of antiques experts, including Thomas Plant and James Lewis, as they appraise interesting and valuable items to take to auction in Sheffield. Among the finds brought in by members of the public are a tortoiseshell locket, a Georgian tankard and a disconcerting electrotherapy kit. Paul also meets two women who were recently awarded honours for keeping the citys steel mills running during the Second World War

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith face off, demonstrating the general knowledge and trivia skills that made them champions last time

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Owain Wyn Evans, Greg Rutherford and Abby Cook on their knowledge of the Somerset town of Taunton

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs representing central England serve their canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry, with Michelin-starred chef Spencer Metzger as this weeks veteran judge. Highlights include a Jerusalem artichoke and turnip dish linked to War Horse and a monochrome, art-deco styled monkfish dish that pays homage to Birminghams Electric Theatre. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Just outside Leicester, Alice joins a team of archaeologists who are investigating a mysterious mound thought to be an Iron Age hillfort, but what they unearth tells a rich story of bandits, religious crusades and connections across the medieval world. In Cambridge, the dig diary cameras are present to witness archaeologists unearthing an extraordinary array of exquisite artefacts from an early Anglo-Saxon cemetery

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya begins in Germanys Bavarian mountains, paragliding over Neuschwanstein Castle before travelling to meet the mayor of Kallstadt, the ancestral home of the Trump family, and heading to Ramstein, Americas largest airbase in Europe Ramstein. Crossing to Spain, Katya begins in the region of Catalonia, discovering how its strong nationalist identity ties into the legacy of dictator Francisco Franco

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Ross Noble, Sara Pascoe and Sally Phillips join regular panellist Alan Davies on the comedy quiz, with host Sandi Toksvig asking very varied questions

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events, presented by Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: In the early hours of a Saturday morning a woman calls 999 to report that she had been attacked and raped by a stranger in a public phone box in Birmingham. Crime scene co-ordinator Jo Ward directs a detailed examination of the phone box for any evidence that may help police identify the attacker, with an investigation being launched by West Midlands Polices specialist Rape and Serious Sexual Offences team, led by Detective Inspector Emma Watson

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian heads to Denmark, where she learns how to build from a Lego master, makes a roof out of seaweed with a group of thatchers, meets a maritime tattoo artist and finds out about sail-making

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Lord Sugar sends the candidates to Egypt, tasking them with organising corporate away days for paying clients. While one team embarks on a desert adventure that ends up being a bumpy ride, the others head out on a lagoon tour which proves to be less than relaxing

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the Egypt task, the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: Tensions run high as Jo and Freya meet for the first time in months. Both want to reconcile, but neither wants to lose ground. Meanwhile, at the behest of the Olivias, Abe has been roped into staging his and Freyas surprise proposal as the first big piece of content for their disability wedding and bridal influencer campaign. Starring Kyla Harris and Elena Samuel

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: As Marcuss time in Saint Remy nears its end, he is invited to a farm party - but he has to bring a dish. In need of inspiration for a celebratory, but simple, recipe, he calls on local farmer Sebastien, who supplies vegetable boxes for his customers, and visits a restaurant where he learns how to make a famous local artichoke dish

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Paranormal: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary the most chilling and unexplainable paranormal activity footage captured by eyewitnesses

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Monster in the Attic, Houston UFO and Utah Bigfoot
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things caught on film. This episode features a video showing a strange formation of flickering lights and a mysterious humanoid creature caught on camera in an abandoned attic

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Chupacabra, Greatest UFO Photo Ever and Mars Sounds
programme.desc: An examination of a recent caught-on-camera sighting of Chupacabra outside an Amarillo Zoo. Plus, whether a photograph taken over Costa Rica the greatest UFO picture ever taken

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Mystery Bird Monster, Ancient Alien Satellite and Behemoth of the Sea Bottom
programme.desc: A strange creature captured by a security camera in Youngstown, Ohio. The bizarre footage leaves residents and experts scratching their heads as they try to identify the mysterious beast. Plus, a strange, deafening sound that echoed across the entire Equatorial Pacific Ocean

programme.title: Irish Pickers
programme.desc: Ian and Butzy visit Mayo in the west of Ireland. They visit Mulranny, go fishing at Clew Bay and stop off at Swinford, before discovering something fascinating in Charlestown

programme.title: Irish Pickers
programme.desc: Ian arrives on his home turf of Dublin. After making a bit profit on an antique clock, he takes a trip to Waterford to bid on some of its world famous crystal

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Welwyn Garden City sort the treasures from their trash, offering them the chance to put items up for auction or accept a cash sum

programme.title: Canadian Pickers

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry, Allen and Guy finally take possession of the Buick Roadmaster they worked so hard to own. After a little tinkering they cruise down to a classic car show in Newbury, where they end up doing a little wheeling and dealing

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.desc: After Seth rescinds his invasive search policies for his employees, the women to return to work. The leader soon wrestles with a new problem - profits are down, so he looks at creative ways to get new customers into the store

programme.desc: The staff receive complaints from customers that a staff member has been letting customers cut in line in exchange for a tip. They later find out that Anton, who works the pawn desk, is responsible

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants - Alan, Kiera, Sue and Gary - pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant invites a line-up of famous faces to take their place in the hotseat in a bid to win as much money as possible for their chosen charities. The contestants comprise astrologer and Celebrity Fit Club graduate Russell Grant alongside former Three Degrees singer Sheila Ferguson, followed by MP Ann Widdecombe and broadcaster Piers Morgan

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Ben, Theo, Tobias and Niamh try to find the right path across the bridge in the hope of winning some cash. Game show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers hosts as five dealers bid against each other to buy items brought in by the public, including a Palestinian ceremonial headdress, vintage school gym steps and typesetting blocks

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis invites five school friends from Kent to answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Adam, Janice, John and Deanne pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Bullseye
programme.desc: Quiz that tests contestants skill at darts and their general knowledge

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

programme.title: Bullseye
programme.desc: Quiz that tests contestants skill at darts and their general knowledge

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Dating show in which singletons meet up with possible partners at a restaurant in the hope of finding love, before deciding whether they want to take things further. Newly married Fred Sirieix watches on as serial dater Conner, from Surrey, tries to connect with tech consultant Charlie, a self-described geek who turns heads. Next in is Sophie, a tattoo-loving mum-of-three from Cheshire who has never dated a woman before. Her date, Emily, is a massage therapist with a calming energy. The two bond quickly, but a twist lies in store as it becomes apparent that one has her husband in tow

programme.title: Freaky (2020)
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old Millie Kessler spends her days trying to survive high school and the cruel actions of the popular crowd. But when she becomes the latest target of the Butcher, the towns infamous serial killer, her senior year becomes the least of her worries. When the Butchers mystical dagger causes him and Millie to magically switch bodies, the frightened teen learns she has just 24 hours to get her identity back before she looks like a middle-aged maniac for ever. Comedy horror, starring Vince Vaughn and Kathryn Newton

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jerseys Spanish Pavillion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eaterys fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Three spooky tales for the shows 30th Halloween special, including a parody of Stranger Things, and a dead Homers spirit trying on some new bodies for size

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa schemes to get her own back on Bart for destroying the prize tomato she grew for a science project. Meanwhile, Homer is arrested for drink-driving and finds himself having to attend Alcoholics Anonymous meetings

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tires of being followed everywhere by a lovestruck Ralph and hurts his feelings by blurting out in public that she cannot stand him - prompting disgruntled local lawman Chief Wiggum to harass Homer in retaliation

programme.title: Jamie Cooks Spring
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver goes through the year, celebrating seasonal ingredients and what can be made with them as they come into season in the UK. He goes on a delicious journey through the kitchen calendar, highlighting weird, wonderful and wonky produce with inspiring recipes for when theyre at their best. He begins by picking some rhubarb from the garden and making a lip-smacking sauce for marinated pork belly, pan-fried until crispy in his hot and sour rhubarb crispy pork noodles, a special dish for any springtime occasion. He picks some new season spinach for a luxurious spinach and goats cheese risotto, before cooking up a scruffy spring tart using leeks. Jamie also demonstrates how to divide shop-bought basil into lots of individual plants to last through the year and celebrates the vibrantly coloured stalks and leaves of chard in a crowd-pleasing cheesy spring cannelloni

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp is enlisted to help identical twins Matt and Chris Wickham buy their first home in north London, but a budget of £350,000 may not be enough to secure a property in their favoured areas of Pimlico and Victoria. Meanwhile, Phil Spencer offers advice to Sam Taylor and his fiancee Krystle Maher, who are keen to move from their rented flat in Deptford into a place of their own

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: A performance by childrens entertainer Nanny Gee in honour of Fredericks second birthday helps to shed some light on Frasiers romantic past, but leaves Lilith in a jealous rage. Comedy, guest starring Emma Thompson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: A Diminished Rebecca with a Suspended Cliff
programme.desc: Rebecca claims to Sam that she can run the bar for a night just as efficiently as he, but has to reassess her boast when she discovers that she has forgotten to renew the liquor licence. Meanwhile, Woody adopts the role of cardsharp when a scorching game of poker develops in the poolroom

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca is left in charge of the bar while the others play poker but soon realises shes out of her depth when she discovers the liquor licence has expired. Bar-room comedy, starring Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on day three of the Games in Italy. Menna Fitzpatrick, Hester Poole, Sam Cozens, Fred Warburton and Neil Simpson are among the British hopefuls in the super-G finals at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. Plus, Great Britain take on South Korea in the round robin stage of the mixed team wheelchair curling at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on day three of the Games, as the super-G finals and South Korea v Great Britain in the round robin stage of the mixed team curling both continue at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre and Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium respectively

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Midday news summary

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Further live coverage of day three of the Games, including Italy v Great Britain in the round robin stage of the wheelchair curling mixed doubles at Cortina Olympic Curling Stadium. The British pairing of Jason Kean and Jo Butterfield are both making their Winter Paralympic debut, but Kean was part of the England team that qualified for the Wheelchair Curling World Championship in 2025, and Butterfield won a gold at the Summer Paralympics in the club throw back in 2016 in Rio

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Jen and Nick from Essex set up a new charity for retired sports horses in France, while Brian and Paula from Devon fight back against the odds to give French life a second shot. Driven by his dream of living by the beach, coffee bar owner James brings his British business idea to Spain

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This weeks competition begins at cosy pub the Courtyard in Falkirk, where guests pay a visit to the Falkirk Wheel, the worlds only rotating boat lift

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, palliative care nurse and never a dull moment Pauline, is hoping to impress her guests with a family-style dinner party and plenty of home comforts. But Pauline kicks off her night by slipping up and forgetting to serve professional singer Danny, while cabaret practitioner Vanessa reveals an unexpected fear and jack-of-all-trades Richard is not impressed. Dental nurse Amy is intrigued by what Pauline does and her caring nature points the group to the answer. And a rousing choral performance that gets everyone involved delivers yet another side of Pauline. Will her snug soiree put her in the driving seat for the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Highlights
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan presents all the key action from day three of the Games in Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. A number of super-G finals took place at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, and there was plenty of British involvement in the round robin stages of the wheelchair curling competitions at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Susan Murray, MP for Mid Dunbartonshire, sets out the Liberal Democrats campaign for a new health centre in Milngavie

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: In Oxfordshire, Joe and Suzanne help the Sellers family salvage their mealtimes from the chaos of long working hours and daily post-commute supermarket trips. At the top of Joe and Suzannes list of goals is getting dad Rudi to relinquish control of the kitchen and demonstrate how much time and money one organised weekly shop will save them

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Theres fireworks ahead as some of the cuffed pairs grapple with being chained to their total opposite. Horse trainer and countryside campaigner Claire, whos 48, is a fierce advocate of hard work. Her handcuffed partner, 29-year-old city-loving model and content creator Bambi loves the easy life. Can Bambi keep up with Claires exhausting work schedule? In London, Green Party councillor Frank, whos 27, and housing lawyer Lin, whos 38, seem to be getting along famously. But Franks in for a shock that leaves him speechless

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Amateur footage and eyewitness accounts telling the story of the earthquake and subsequent tsunami that caused devastation in Japan in March 2011. The film also discovers how the country dealt with the aftermath of these natural disasters, as people struggled to rebuild their lives and communities

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: Professor Asif Chaudry attempts to remove a tumour between 74-year-old Matthews oesophagus and stomach. Surgical oncologist Myles Smith tackles 30-year-old Laurens myxoid-liposarcoma, a tumour in her groin which has been causing her problems since the age of eight

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Essex and Herts Air Ambulance rush to help headteacher Andy, who has collapsed after a cardiac arrest at home. In Ipswich, warehouse employee Rob is trapped under a pallet of granite and needs the critical care skills of the East Anglian Air Ambulance Service to help with his broken leg. In Abingdon, the Thames Valley Air Ambulance team try to understand the cause of 13-week pregnant Katies seizures and in Rayleigh the crew help Jo, who has been lying on her bathroom floor for nine hours with a broken ankle

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for West Babylon, New York, to help the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts. The chef struggles to cope with disorganised staff and an unappealing menu, but quickly realises the real problem lies in the kitchen, where a lack of leadership and communication is causing serious trouble

programme.title: No Other Land (2024)
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insiders view of the destruction of homes and villages in the West Bank, written and directed by a Palestinian-Israeli collective of activist film-makers - consisting of Basel Adra, Yuval Abraham, Hamdan Ballal and Rachel Szor.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help find a property for first-time buyers Mike Richie and girlfriend Katy Roberts, who have recently returned from a 10-month period of globetrotting and want to settle down in Leeds. Meanwhile, Ann-Marie McCullough and partner Lee Connolly hope to swap life in the city for a three-bedroom period home, so the experts take them to Ilkley in West Yorkshire, and Harrogate in North Yorkshire

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Vickys still on edge after recent events, while Sienna makes a tough decision. And after months of torment and pain, Dodger finally has his chance to expose Sully for what he really is, but Sullys ready to fight dirty. Donnys family support him as he faces the truth

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Woodys friends are dismayed to find his English jaunt has turned him into a snob, while Frasier has problems with fitness trainer Sam. Starring Ted Danson, Woody Harrelson and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm comes into money, so Rebecca urges a reluctant Sam to bring up the subject of his substantial bar tab. Meanwhile, Frasier encourages Lilith to confront her overbearing mother

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Take Me Out of the Ball Game
programme.desc: Sam considers a return to playing professional baseball, only to discover his age is against him, while Frasier loses Liliths favourite lab rat when he brings it to the bar

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening events on day four of the Games in Italy. The super-combined get under way with the super-G discipline at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, an event that also includes the slalom and has seen Menna Fitzpatrick and Neil Simpson medal for ParalympicsGB in recent Games. Plus, Dame Sarah Storey reports from Great Britains round robin match against Italy in the mixed team event at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Further live coverage of day four from Italy, including the conclusion of Great Britain v Italy in the round robin stage of the mixed team curling at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium. Plus, cross-country skiing action at Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Continued live coverage of day four, including the slalom stage of the super combined events at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. There are five British participants bidding for glory in these events - past medallists Menna Fitzpatrick and Neil Simpson, newcomer to the sport Neil Warburton and teenagers Hester Poole and Sam Cozens

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Billy Monger presents the concluding live coverage of day four in Italy, including the wheelchair curling mixed doubles semi-finals from Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium, as the four remaining pairs contest a place in tomorrow afternoons gold medal match

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Colin Jackson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Relocating in peak season costs coffee bar owner James time and money in Spain and in France, Jen and Nick have to dig deep to keep their horse charity going. Opening day comes round fast for Brian and Paula at their new French restaurant. Will they be ready in time?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals arrive at Finn Village, Cliff and Monikas countryside place near Loch Lomond, where one guest decides too much nature can be a bad thing in their jungle-themed room. A tranquil boat trip on the loch should be just the ticket to help the hoteliers relax - so its a shame when relations start getting choppy out on the water

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second host in Manchester is plumber and adult merchandise director Richard, who is cooking a rich and indulgent menu and making everything from scratch in the hope of a few extra points. However, Danny gets more than he bargained for when he and Vanessa find some of Richards underwear samples from work

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Highlights
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan presents action from day four of the Games, held in Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. The super-combined events took place at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, with a host of ParalympicsGB stars involved. Medals were also handed out in Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium at Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium, and the mixed doubles semi-finals took place at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Sioned Williams, Member of the Senedd for South Wales West, sets out Plaid Cymrus childcare policy

programme.title: Battle of the Brands
programme.sub-title: Aldi vs MandS: Battle of the Brands
programme.desc: Kate Quilton presents the story of the supermarket showdown between two of the biggest brands in the business, with insiders revealing how much the two retailers now overlap, despite starting out at opposite ends of the spending spectrum. Its a story of price wars over posh ready meals, wine, flowers and copycat cakes - and even courtroom battles over gin

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Some of the pairs have had enough and called it quits. But not 70-year-old Yorkshireman Bob and 38-year-old London youth worker Chris. However, when the latter learns about a shocking incident from his partners past, he wonder whether he can continue. Meanwhile, eco-warrior and vegan campaigner Morag is shocked when the woman she is chained to, petrol-head and beauty salon owner Angie, reveals her views on climate change. Hosted by Jonathan Ross

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions to Race Across the World, The Last of Us, Married at First Sight Australia, WrestleMania, Chess Masters: The Endgame, Love on the Spectrum, Secrets of the Penguins and This Morning

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on The Apprentice, Britains Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, Alexander Armstrong in India and ITV News about the conflict in Iran

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Another group of curious committed couples explore the possibility of opening up their relationships with sexually liberated residents. In this episode, builder Connor and full-time mother Kirstie are interested in bringing another man into their relationship and want to start the process by having a threesome. However, after connecting with resident Nathan, things go off the rails due to Connors confidence issues. Meanwhile, married parents Chanice and Richardo, who are torn between a threesome or foursome, face an unexpected issue - finding a resident who is attracted to them

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eaterys co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by Jamali Maddix, Vittorio Angelone and Penny Mordaunt for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag #isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Comedian and podcaster Adam Buxton joins Joe Lycett to assess the wonders of Prague, capital of the Czech Republic. A kayak tour along the River Vltava allows them to take in the key sights, including the castle and the Charles Bridge. They then explore the work of sculptor David Cerný, whose controversial pieces adorn the city. Dinner is served at Levitate, where they eat their way through an 18-course tasting menu, including eating a sausage out of a skull and sampling caviar-laced ice-cream. On day two, the pair enjoy a beer spa before ascending the Zizkov TV Tower, for a birds eye view of the city

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Outreach manager Gill and accountant Mark kick off the competition in Glasgow with a night of comforting food and even more comfortable company. On day two, mischievous Derek pushes Matts buttons and ends up being banished from the kitchen. On the final evening, a cooking disaster sees Cat blowing her top, but Martin saves the day

programme.title: Freaky (2020)
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old Millie Kessler spends her days trying to survive high school and the cruel actions of the popular crowd. But when she becomes the latest target of the Butcher, the towns infamous serial killer, her senior year becomes the least of her worries. When the Butchers mystical dagger causes him and Millie to magically switch bodies, the frightened teen learns she has just 24 hours to get her identity back before she looks like a middle-aged maniac for ever. Comedy horror, starring Vince Vaughn and Kathryn Newton
