The film also discovers how the country dealt with the aftermath of these natural disasters, as people struggled to rebuild their lives and communities

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: Professor Asif Chaudry attempts to remove a tumour between 74-year-old Matthews oesophagus and stomach. Surgical oncologist Myles Smith tackles 30-year-old Laurens myxoid-liposarcoma, a tumour in her groin which has been causing her problems since the age of eight

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Essex and Herts Air Ambulance rush to help headteacher Andy, who has collapsed after a cardiac arrest at home. In Ipswich, warehouse employee Rob is trapped under a pallet of granite and needs the critical care skills of the East Anglian Air Ambulance Service to help with his broken leg. In Abingdon, the Thames Valley Air Ambulance team try to understand the cause of 13-week pregnant Katies seizures and in Rayleigh the crew help Jo, who has been lying on her bathroom floor for nine hours with a broken ankle

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for West Babylon, New York, to help the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts. The chef struggles to cope with disorganised staff and an unappealing menu, but quickly realises the real problem lies in the kitchen, where a lack of leadership and communication is causing serious trouble

programme.title: No Other Land (2024)
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insiders view of the destruction of homes and villages in the West Bank, written and directed by a Palestinian-Israeli collective of activist film-makers - consisting of Basel Adra, Yuval Abraham, Hamdan Ballal and Rachel Szor.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help find a property for first-time buyers Mike Richie and girlfriend Katy Roberts, who have recently returned from a 10-month period of globetrotting and want to settle down in Leeds. Meanwhile, Ann-Marie McCullough and partner Lee Connolly hope to swap life in the city for a three-bedroom period home, so the experts take them to Ilkley in West Yorkshire, and Harrogate in North Yorkshire

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Vickys still on edge after recent events, while Sienna makes a tough decision. And after months of torment and pain, Dodger finally has his chance to expose Sully for what he really is, but Sullys ready to fight dirty. Donnys family support him as he faces the truth

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Woodys friends are dismayed to find his English jaunt has turned him into a snob, while Frasier has problems with fitness trainer Sam. Starring Ted Danson, Woody Harrelson and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm comes into money, so Rebecca urges a reluctant Sam to bring up the subject of his substantial bar tab. Meanwhile, Frasier encourages Lilith to confront her overbearing mother

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Take Me Out of the Ball Game
programme.desc: Sam considers a return to playing professional baseball, only to discover his age is against him, while Frasier loses Liliths favourite lab rat when he brings it to the bar

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening events on day four of the Games in Italy. The super-combined get under way with the super-G discipline at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, an event that also includes the slalom and has seen Menna Fitzpatrick and Neil Simpson medal for ParalympicsGB in recent Games. Plus, Dame Sarah Storey reports from Great Britains round robin match against Italy in the mixed team event at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Further live coverage of day four from Italy, including the conclusion of Great Britain v Italy in the round robin stage of the mixed team curling at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium. Plus, cross-country skiing action at Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Continued live coverage of day four, including the slalom stage of the super combined events at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. There are five British participants bidding for glory in these events - past medallists Menna Fitzpatrick and Neil Simpson, newcomer to the sport Neil Warburton and teenagers Hester Poole and Sam Cozens

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Billy Monger presents the concluding live coverage of day four in Italy, including the wheelchair curling mixed doubles semi-finals from Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium, as the four remaining pairs contest a place in tomorrow afternoons gold medal match

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Colin Jackson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Relocating in peak season costs coffee bar owner James time and money in Spain and in France, Jen and Nick have to dig deep to keep their horse charity going. Opening day comes round fast for Brian and Paula at their new French restaurant. Will they be ready in time?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals arrive at Finn Village, Cliff and Monikas countryside place near Loch Lomond, where one guest decides too much nature can be a bad thing in their jungle-themed room. A tranquil boat trip on the loch should be just the ticket to help the hoteliers relax - so its a shame when relations start getting choppy out on the water

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second host in Manchester is plumber and adult merchandise director Richard, who is cooking a rich and indulgent menu and making everything from scratch in the hope of a few extra points. However, Danny gets more than he bargained for when he and Vanessa find some of Richards underwear samples from work

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Highlights
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan presents action from day four of the Games, held in Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. The super-combined events took place at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, with a host of ParalympicsGB stars involved. Medals were also handed out in Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium at Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium, and the mixed doubles semi-finals took place at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Sioned Williams, Member of the Senedd for South Wales West, sets out Plaid Cymrus childcare policy

programme.title: Battle of the Brands
programme.sub-title: Aldi vs MandS: Battle of the Brands
programme.desc: Kate Quilton presents the story of the supermarket showdown between two of the biggest brands in the business, with insiders revealing how much the two retailers now overlap, despite starting out at opposite ends of the spending spectrum. Its a story of price wars over posh ready meals, wine, flowers and copycat cakes - and even courtroom battles over gin

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Some of the pairs have had enough and called it quits. But not 70-year-old Yorkshireman Bob and 38-year-old London youth worker Chris. However, when the latter learns about a shocking incident from his partners past, he wonder whether he can continue. Meanwhile, eco-warrior and vegan campaigner Morag is shocked when the woman she is chained to, petrol-head and beauty salon owner Angie, reveals her views on climate change. Hosted by Jonathan Ross

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions to Race Across the World, The Last of Us, Married at First Sight Australia, WrestleMania, Chess Masters: The Endgame, Love on the Spectrum, Secrets of the Penguins and This Morning

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on The Apprentice, Britains Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, Alexander Armstrong in India and ITV News about the conflict in Iran

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Another group of curious committed couples explore the possibility of opening up their relationships with sexually liberated residents. In this episode, builder Connor and full-time mother Kirstie are interested in bringing another man into their relationship and want to start the process by having a threesome. However, after connecting with resident Nathan, things go off the rails due to Connors confidence issues. Meanwhile, married parents Chanice and Richardo, who are torn between a threesome or foursome, face an unexpected issue - finding a resident who is attracted to them

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eaterys co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by Jamali Maddix, Vittorio Angelone and Penny Mordaunt for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag #isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Comedian and podcaster Adam Buxton joins Joe Lycett to assess the wonders of Prague, capital of the Czech Republic. A kayak tour along the River Vltava allows them to take in the key sights, including the castle and the Charles Bridge. They then explore the work of sculptor David Cerný, whose controversial pieces adorn the city. Dinner is served at Levitate, where they eat their way through an 18-course tasting menu, including eating a sausage out of a skull and sampling caviar-laced ice-cream. On day two, the pair enjoy a beer spa before ascending the Zizkov TV Tower, for a birds eye view of the city

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Outreach manager Gill and accountant Mark kick off the competition in Glasgow with a night of comforting food and even more comfortable company. On day two, mischievous Derek pushes Matts buttons and ends up being banished from the kitchen. On the final evening, a cooking disaster sees Cat blowing her top, but Martin saves the day

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj restore a 163-year-old derelict chapel on Wales Llyn Peninsula, to turn it into a forever home with a working pottery studio. The pair start renovations on the downstairs Sunday School so it can become their living space, complete with a kitchen, bathroom, bedroom and a snug. But before they can begin they have to rip out the fungus and try to control the dry rot thats infected the chapel

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Uncut
programme.desc: Extended edition. Patsy Palmer, Jack Whitehall, Krishnan Guru-Murthy and Joe Wilkinson join team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock on the irreverent panel show, hosted by Jimmy Carr. The contestants answer questions based on opinion polls carried out around the country to see who best understands what is going on in Britain today

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Improv comedy show with Colin Mochrie, Ryan Stiles, Jeff Davis, Brad Sherwood and Aisha Tyler and a special appearance by Cheryl Hines

programme.title: Jack Dee: So What?
programme.desc: A 2013 performance at Londons Hammersmith Apollo by the comedian, filmed during a tour which saw him return to stand-up after a hiatus of five years. Jack tackles lifes petty annoyances and everyday frustrations with his trademark mix of deadpan cynicism and understated fury, from visitors using his electricity to charge their mobile phones to his neighbours wind chimes and the problems of finding a decent electrician

programme.title: Lee Evans: Big - Live at the O2
programme.desc: A performance by the Bristol-born stand-up comedian, who is renowned for his energetic physical observational work, recorded at Londons O2 at the end of a UK tour

programme.title: Iain Stirlings Roast the Internet
programme.desc: Comedy entertainment show about the very worst things on the internet, with Joe Thomas, London Hughes and Harriet Kemsley joining the host on the first edition to examine social clips, posts and trends

programme.title: The Ricky Gervais Show
programme.desc: Stephen Merchant provides talking-points by reading out fans e-mails, sparking a discussion about a lion that attacked Cambodian fighters. Karl Pilkington also shares his experiences of charitable giving, thoughts on nudist organisations and the pitfalls of gym locker rooms. Plus, another instalment of Monkey News

programme.title: Comedy Game Night
programme.desc: Liza Tarbuck, along with team captains Sue Perkins and Guz Khan, invite guests round to play party games. Tonight features Rufus Hound, Jacqueline Jossa and Melvin Odoom

programme.desc: The guys lend a hand at a Greek restaurant and then compete head-to-head answering questions in a focus group. The loser is forced to spend a day locked in a den at the zoo

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie and Frank accidentally crash their car through the wall of Rays house, causing a bitter family dispute - and forcing Debra into the role of peacemaker. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert believes Amy is the woman of his dreams and decides to tell her the truth about Joanne and Stefania, but she is so shocked by the confession that she refuses to take him back

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray accidentally kills Allys pet hamster while the kids are away from home for the weekend, so Debra insists they give it a dignified funeral. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert is promoted to lieutenant, much to the dismay of Ray, who feels overshadowed and becomes convinced his own prestige is suffering as a result. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Brad Garrett

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray goes to a wedding, where he finds himself regretting a missed opportunity to escort a high-school date to her door, and is tormented by thoughts of what might have been. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with Chandler in a Box
programme.desc: Chandler agrees to spend Thanksgiving in a packing crate as penance for kissing Kathy, and the friends decide to give each other seasonal gifts. Monica visits Richards son, who finds her every bit as attractive as his father did. Comedy, guest starring Michael Vartan

programme.sub-title: The One Where Theyre Going to Party!
programme.desc: Things start looking up for the gang as Monica gets a career opportunity that is too good to turn down and Rachel is offered a promotion. Meanwhile, Ross and Chandler are excited to hear an old friend is coming to visit. Jennifer Aniston and Courteney Cox star

programme.sub-title: The One with the Girl from Poughkeepsie
programme.desc: Ross embarks on a long-distance relationship, Chandler sets Rachel up with one of his colleagues, and Phoebe composes a seasonal song for her friends. Monica has trouble asserting her authority at work and hires Joey so she can fire him in front of the staff

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebes half-brother makes a startling announcement and asks a great favour of her, while Joey attempts to break down social barriers when he takes a job as a tour guide at Rosss museum. Chandler fears not living up to Kathys expectations

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe learns the result of her pregnancy test and the friends competitive spirit gets the better of them in a battle of the sexes arising from an apparently simple challenge. Comedy, starring Lisa Kudrow

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel schemes to get a handsome client to ask her out on a date, while Chandlers girlfriend is driven away by his jealousy. Guest starring Tate Donovan

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: In May 2012, six children aged between five and 13 died at the hands of their parents Mick and Mairead Philpott in a house fire in Derby. programme.title: Homicide Hunter

programme.desc: When part-time stripper Rachel Siani was found dead, investigations initially focused on a former colleague - before attention shifted to an obsessive club patron

programme.desc: How Samantha Nicole Wohlford, along with co-conspirators, orchestrated the kidnapping and murder of her husband, Ernest Lee Ibarra Jr

programme.title: Homicide Hunter

programme.desc: A judge chastises a defendant for sleeping in virtual court, and a grieving mother forgives the man who killed her unborn twins

programme.title: Court Cam

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: The father of Clare Wood discusses how her murder in Salford in 2009 gave birth to a new law allowing people to find out if their partners have an abusive past

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: In May 2012, six children aged between five and 13 died at the hands of their parents Mick and Mairead Philpott in a house fire in Derby. The blaze was started as an attempt to frame the former mistress of father-of-17 Mick, and despite tearful appearances in front of the TV cameras, the couple were convicted of manslaughter months later. This documentary features interviews with eyewitnesses and neighbours who tried to save the children

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: The series returns to take a closer look at the most horrific crimes ever committed on British soil. This instalment recalls the May 2017 Manchester Arena bombing, when a radical Islamist detonated a shrapnel-laden home-made bomb as people were leaving the venue following a concert by the American singer Ariana Grande

programme.desc: Lisa has to go undercover as a nun and infiltrate a convent when Maggie goes missing after being left on its doorstep. Meanwhile, Marge is left temporarily blind when she stares at a total eclipse, so Homer has to sort out their rat-infested kitchen

programme.desc: Homer is forced to eat bland food after he burns his taste buds while trying to copy a fire-eating street performer. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eaterys co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.desc: The family constructs a tennis court in the back garden and Bart becomes an expert player, much to the annoyance of a jealous Homer. Featuring the guest voices of Venus and Serena Williams, Andre Agassi and Pete Sampras

Fearing she will not be taken seriously, she appoints Homer as a figurehead - but he begins taking all the credit for her labours. Meanwhile, Bart is thrilled to discover Principal Skinner has a lethal peanut allergy

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Halloween-inspired tales featuring the all-American family. Featuring the guest voice of Dr Phil

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Bank manager Ross hopes it will be third time lucky as he plays icebreaker games with primary school teacher Sarah-Jayne. Beauty salon owner Kenny is confident he will impress Jess, while sales manager Yemis date looks promising with DJ Chelsea, who shares his love of music. Computer programmer Andrew, who has been single for more than 10 years, meets old-fashioned romantic Sarah

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: When self-proclaimed ladies man Jayden, 20, meets childminder Isabel, 19, things go well between them - until they realise they have a mutual friend. Sarah, 44, has been deaf since birth, and wants to find love with a hearing man, so shes relieved to find 47-year-old estate agent John is easy to lip read. Eternal romantic Tim returns to the restaurant, and his date, Sarah, shares his passion for bad jokes, exploding high fives and limericks. Disco-loving personal trainer Ellis, 47, is pleased when 53-year-old hairdresser Gary turns up, and his touching life story makes her warm to him even more. Meanwhile, Ryan, 33, has recently become single after an 11-year relationship, and turns to alcohol to help him relax

programme.desc: A look back at the times when what started as great chemistry in the studio ended in dating disaster, reliving memorable arguments and cringeworthy moments, including bisexual Leah from the third series, who brought her pet dog Kush to the studio to help her pick her partner. programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In East Anglia, hip-hop enthusiasts Christine and Terry provide a hearty menu for their guests, before allotment owners Tim and Amanda host with a menu dedicated to their home-grown fruit and veg. programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three rival couples battle it out to host the best dinner party in Newcastle. Partners Fiona and Hannah kick things off with their Moroccan-themed menu. programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Laid-back south Londoner Daniel wants to get his wedding grooving like a Caribbean street carnival. However, sensible student bride Sabrina dreams of a more traditional occasion and a wedding her parents will be proud of. While Sabrina pictures a tranquil location, Daniel splashes the cash on trucks and sound systems

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Beauty queen and bride-to-be Yasmin has her heart set on a fairy-tale wedding. Powerlifter Yiannis is taking Yasmins dream to the extreme, but will she be the belle of the ball, or will the nuptials have an ugly twist in the tale?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Lorne Abony, the CEO and chairman of in-store media provider Mood Media, goes undercover in one of the fastest growing companies in America. Adopting a disguise in his own business, he finds out how efficiently it is functioning

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Jim Rogers, chairman and CEO of Kampgrounds of America, goes undercover within his own company to discover whether his employees share his boy-scout philosophy. However, Jims wife Sandy fears his naturally outgoing and cheerful nature could undo his cover story as a recently unemployed reality show contestant

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Rosaria bought Bella Luna to realise her son Gianfrancos ambition to become a chef, but the Italian restaurants future looks bleak due to his lack of experience in the kitchen combined with her refusal to acknowledge the eaterys many flaws. Gordon Ramsay heads to Easton, Pennsylvania, to help the troubled pair and save the business from financial disaster

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Bostons La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Its hammer time in the third series where the property brothers work their makeover magic. Will helping these homeowners be as tough as nails or as easy as flipping a switch? Here come the property brothers, ready to focus on critical elements and house hunting. programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell

programme.desc: The chef arrives in the riverside town of LIsle-sur-la-Sorgue in south-eastern France, where his culinary hero Keith Floyd once owned a restaurant. Heading back in the direction of the town, James samples some truffle-laden dishes at renowned restaurant La Beaugraviere

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Another chance to see the 2012 contest, as presenters Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins invite 12 enthusiastic bakers to take part in a contest to determine which of them is Britains best amateur, facing challenges devised by judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood. To begin with, they tackle an upside down cake, before following Pauls recipe for rum babas, a hybrid of cake and enriched dough that baffles some of the competitors. Finally, they are challenged to produce a show-stopping cake that reveals a hidden design when sliced into. Who will be first to leave?

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The 11 remaining amateur bakers face three tasks designed to test their bread-making skills, beginning by preparing two varieties of flatbread for judges Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry. The hopefuls then tackle a demanding technical challenge as they try to replicate Pauls recipe for an eight-strand plaited loaf, before receiving the dubious honour of becoming the first contestants in the shows history to make bagels. At the end of the day, Paul and Mary must decide who is this rounds Star Baker - and who will be going home. Presented by Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins. From 2012

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: The chef learns that Salem is famous for its doughnuts, as well as its witches, as his culinary journey to discover the best buns and fabulous bakes in America concludes

programme.title: M*A*S*H

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: On his way back from Sleepy Eye, Isaiah Edwards meets up with a dying old man, whose last request is for Isaiah to take care of his three-year-old, Blanche. It isnt until later that Isaiah realises that this little girl is actually an orang-utan. Most of Walnut Grove is happy to welcome Blanche into their community, but the trouble starts when Harriet Oleson gets involved. Elsewhere, Willie Olesons parents are shocked when their son announces he is planning to get married

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: Isaiah makes a promise to a dying man that he will find a home for his little girl, only to realise the infant is a baby orang-utan which wreaks havoc in Walnut Grove. Meanwhile, Willie is finally ready to graduate and propose to his girlfriend, but struggles to find the courage to tell his parents that he does not want to attend college.

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: John-Boy is approached by old flame Selena Linville, who wants him to come with her to Spain and report on the Civil War

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: Part one of two. John-Boy receives an offer from a publishing house just as the house is nearly destroyed in a fire - and his manuscript is lost in the blaze

programme.title: Ballykissangel
programme.desc: The village faces up to Assumptas tragic death, and a heartbroken Peter has to decide whether his future lies in Ballykissangel or elsewhere, away from constant reminders of the woman he loved. Drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Tony Doyle and Tina Kellegher

programme.title: Born and Bred
programme.desc: The past comes back to haunt Wilf when a former German soldier turns up in the village with news of his brother. Arthur creates havoc in hospital after spraining his ankle, and an artist sets hearts fluttering among the women of Ormston. Drama, starring James Bolam, Michael French and John Henshaw

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: A distraught Dr Napier berates herself for failing to diagnose a case of diphtheria, while Dr Cameron shoulders the melancholy task of attending an old friend who is hovering at deaths door. Drama, starring David Rintoul

programme.title: Rumpole of the Bailey
programme.desc: An invitation for the barrister to spend the weekend at a castle turns into a plea for legal help when the lord of the manor reveals hes been accused of drowning an old woman on the estate. Courtroom drama, with Leo McKern

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Harry Morgan guests as batty General Bartford Hamilton Steele, who mistakes Klinger for his wife, Marjorie. Morgan won an Emmy for his performance in this episode of the Korean War comedy, also starring Jamie Farr and Alan Alda

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Frank jeopardises delicate negotiations with the Chinese for the exchange of PoW patients by packing a gun - but the American-educated enemy doctor sees the funny side. Loudon Wainwright III guest stars

programme.desc: Frank bullies Hawkeye into standing in as officer of the day in Henrys absence. Korean War comedy, starring Alan Alda and Larry Linville

programme.title: M*A*S*H

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Hawkeye struggles in vain to save a soldiers life as the OR fills with wounded combatants. Korean War comedy, starring Alan Alda and Gary Burghoff

programme.title: M*A*S*H

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: The series acknowledges the inspiration of Joseph Hellers novel Catch-22 in an episode in which Trappers ulcer buys him a ticket home, only to have his farewell party spoiled by the announcement of new regulations forcing him to stay. Comedy, starring Gary Burghoff and Wayne Rogers

programme.title: The New Avengers
programme.desc: After receiving a strange message alerting them to danger, Steed researches some old files while Purdey and Gambit investigate the whereabouts of legendary agent, who has resurfaced after 17 years. programme.title: Little House: Look Back to Yesterday (1983)

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Walton children are sent to live with different families while their house is rebuilt

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: John-Boy prepares to publish the first edition of his newspaper, and is tempted to report on a political scandal when he witnesses the mayor crash his car

programme.title: Ballykissangel
programme.desc: The auction of Fitzgeralds bar could see eager bidder Brian Quigley pipped at the post by the wealthy Bella Mooney, but his daughter Niamh proves unenthusiastic about running Assumptas old pub. Meanwhile, the replacement priest arrives, accompanied by his globetrotting sister. Pauline McLynn (Father Ted) guest stars

programme.title: Born and Bred
programme.desc: Wilf and Arthur get in a flap with a rum-drinking parrot, and the big day arrives in Ormston - but the groom may not be all he seems. Tom offers to help with the wedding arrangements, but his time is eaten up when a farmer hurts his arm in a threshing machine, leading the farm labourers to strike for improved working conditions

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: The medic temporarily hangs up his stethoscope to intervene in a confrontation between Irish farm labourers and irate locals who blame them for a typhus outbreak, resolving matters with a combination of tough talking and common sense. David Rintoul stars, with Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Rumpole of the Bailey
programme.desc: The crusty barrister is charged by Judge Oliphant with contempt of court and faces disbarment, so Hilda persuades Sam Ballard to defend him. Courtroom drama, with Leo McKern, Marion Mathie and Peter Blythe

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Henry frets that his wife might betray him, and Father Mulcahy remains steady in the faith when asked to preside over a Jewish circumcision ceremony for a Korean-born war baby. Comedy, starring William Christopher

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: In Henrys absence, Frank declares the 4077th a dry camp, prompting a rebellion from the seasoned drinkers - especially Hawkeye and Trapper. Korean War comedy, starring Alan Alda and Gary Burghoff

programme.sub-title: There Is Nothing Like a Nurse
programme.desc: Hawkeye and Trapper try to raise flagging spirits after the nurses are evacuated because of the threat of an enemy parachute drop. Comedy, guest starring Loudon Wainwright III, with Alan Alda

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: The further romantic misadventures of the charming painter, played by Karl Howman. Jackos interest in football is revived - much to Eric and Jeans amusement

programme.desc: Jacko loses his home and his job, and things look so desperate that for a moment an offer from Elmo of free board and lodging above the wine bar looks tempting

programme.desc: Jacko loses his home and his job, and things look so desperate that for a moment an offer from Elmo of free board and lodging above the wine bar looks tempting

programme.title: The New Avengers
programme.desc: Burt Brandon is released from prison after stealing top secret information that was never found, so Steeds team try to track him down. However, it looks like they are not the only people who are on his trail

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Tara battle a group of rogue scientists trying to sell a deadly invention - a virus that causes people to sneeze themselves to death. Fulton Mackay (Porridge) guest stars

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Henry frets that his wife might betray him, and Father Mulcahy remains steady in the faith when asked to preside over a Jewish circumcision ceremony for a Korean-born war baby. Comedy, starring William Christopher

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: In Henrys absence, Frank declares the 4077th a dry camp, prompting a rebellion from the seasoned drinkers - especially Hawkeye and Trapper. Korean War comedy, starring Alan Alda and Gary Burghoff

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.sub-title: There Is Nothing Like a Nurse
programme.desc: Hawkeye and Trapper try to raise flagging spirits after the nurses are evacuated because of the threat of an enemy parachute drop. Comedy, guest starring Loudon Wainwright III, with Alan Alda

programme.title: Home to Roost
programme.desc: Matthew feigns illness to avoid a fight with a boxer whose girlfriend he has been dating. Ray Winstone guest stars

programme.title: Good News on GREAT! Extra
programme.desc: An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world

programme.title: Highway to Heaven
programme.desc: Mark returns to his old police precinct, but is shocked to hear the racist views expressed by a former colleague

programme.title: Good News on GREAT! Extra
programme.desc: An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world

programme.title: Highway to Heaven
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jonathan learns that his widow has died, but when he is refused permission to join her in Heaven, he rebels against God. Dorothy McGuire guest stars

programme.title: Signs of a Psychopath
programme.desc: A woman awakens to find her two young sons with their throats cut. Their aunts boyfriend confesses to his mother, and she wears a wire to capture her son

programme.title: Signs of a Psychopath

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show as contestant Jay tries to open the 22 red boxes in the right order, taking on the infamous Banker for the chance to win a life-changing cash prize

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

In the Urgent Treatment Unit, 79-year-old John is being patched up after being run over by a car at his local market

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics find a woman whos fallen, is on blood thinners and may have a bleed on the brain. A man is finding it hard to communicate and his face is drooped on the right-hand side; all tell-tale signs of a possible stroke. The family of a baby with a complex medical history ring for help when his heart begins beating extremely fast

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The stories of people who changed the course of their lives by taking huge risks, including a consultant who swapped an army career for the frontline of emergency medicine. programme.title: The Perfect Pitch

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Married couple Mo and Anthony seek a holiday apartment with a sea view in Torremolinos on Spains Costa del Sol. Laura Hamilton is tasked with showing them the best of what their £130,000 budget can get them. However, whether any of the five properties the property expert has up her sleeve will result in an offer remains to be seen

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Jen is the first person to stage for a week of dinner parties in Glasgow, serving up a pink-themed evening with a straightforward menu to impress her guests. She needs to pull out all the stops to impress partnerships manager Dorine, food vlogger Darren, psychic medium Robert and nail technician Kasia of her virues as host

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Psychic medium Robert is the second to host in and around Glasgow, hoping to wow his guests with a glimpse into their futures and a menu replicating his wedding meal in Turkey

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Glasgow food vlogger Darren serves up a menu his guests are unlikely to forget in a hurry as he is deep frying everything on it, including lasagne, lettuce and chocolate bars

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Next to host in and around the Glasgow area is 28-year-old partnerships manager Dorine, who opts for a bit of sparkle with glitter and gold, and a French-inspired menu sprinkled with flavours of her childhood. However, tensions between Darren and Kasia remain

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Last to host in and around the Glasgow area is Polish nail technician Kasia, who hopes to scoop the £1,000 prize with an evening of traditional Polish food. It proves an entertaining night with a lively game of Polish Horseshoes, boys versus girls knocking bottles over with frisbees and confessions of phobias around the table leaving everyone open-mouthed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off in rural Co Durham at Bowlees Farm Holiday Cottages, where married couple Tony and Viv Smith hope that their tranquil setting, private hot tubs and indoor swimming pool will impress their visitors. Their guests are bowled over by the views and the facilities, but friends Jane and Rachel find fault with the cleanliness in the kitchen. At dinner, its revealed that breakfast will be provided by the local farm shop, the only facility that Tony and Viv dont seem to have. Despite the clean plates, married couple Amanda and Howard make their feelings clear on the lack of options

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the Piebald Inn in Hunmanby, North Yorkshire, where the lack of a thermostat and a divisive design idea irritate some of the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Stoneleigh House Hotel, a boutique townhouse in the heart of Buckingham run by Jane Parslow and Rachel Rawding. At dinner, the guests are filled in about the Piebalds main attraction, but Jane and Viv dont see pie to pie about a design idea. The group members have a mixed nights sleep, but they all agree about the quality of the breakfast - which is topped off by a surprise from John

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final venue is glamping site Evenlode Grounds in the Cotswolds, owned by Howard and Amanda Wooliams, who impress their guests by revealing it is also a working farm

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners meet for the last time to find out what theyve been paid and to settle some scores. First up to fire back on their feedback are Tony and Viv, followed by John and Lesley, who are both desperate to know why Jane and Rachel were the only two who wouldnt return to their BandBs

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: The BMW 2002 Turbo was the first European production car to have a turbo-charged engine. It also happens to be on Tim Shaws all-time favourite list. The vehicle in question belongs to 79-year-old boy racer Raymond from the West Midlands, who has owned the car, which was built in 1974, for over 30 years. But Ray now has health problems and his beloved car has ended up rusting on his drive. When Tim and Fuzz Townshend get the vehicle to the workshop, they discover that its going to be even more work than they were expecting

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a Welsh motoring enthusiasts beloved Vauxhall Astra back to its former glory

programme.title: The Dog House Australia
programme.desc: Three-year-old Ollie and his family search for a forever friend. Can Frankie the pup help Simon overcome a childhood dog attack trauma? Jaran has the chance to become Liz and Scotts first dog

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin meets Mike and Sarah, who bought a remote smallholding in the Derbyshire Dales, somewhere to rear animals and provide a home for themselves and their elderly parents. Kevin follows the couple as they set out to build a 21st-century version of a historic Derbyshire longhouse. Enormous, corrugated and with a highly complex roof shape, it was never going to be an easy build. But soon they face delays and their schedule begins to slide. However, that is nothing compared with the problems of a more personal nature that hit the project

programme.title: Our Farm Next Door: Amanda, Clive and Kids
programme.desc: As Christmas looms, Amanda and family face re-roofing Anty Johns during a fearsome Dales winter, which follows a wet and stormy autumn that has left the project months behind schedule. Builder Richard and roofer Steven must do their best to complete the arduous and technical challenge of positioning dozens of stone slabs, while trying to stay dry and warm

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A builder falls at work and dislocates his shoulder in such an unusual way it takes a five-strong team to pull it into place. Alan, who is in his sixties and had one leg amputated in 2009, comes in with the other dangerously swollen following a holiday in Goa, and 83-year-old James has to be persuaded by senior sister AnnMarie to accept treatment for a cyst on his knee

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old Josh is flown in with a possible penile fracture resulting from a canal barge accident, and while his parents make jokes to try to lighten the atmosphere, the injury could have serious repercussions for his future. A man with an injured finger following a night of clubbing contemplates his upcoming gender reassignment therapy, which will include taking hormones, while an 80-year-old with terminal cancer arrives after collapsing at her home

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman whose defibrillator located inside her chest has just delivered an electric shock to her heart and is worried that shes having a heart attack. A man falls on his driveway, resulting in a massive swelling over his eye, and a patient who has recently had bowel surgery is worried that he may have an infection

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A builder falls at work and dislocates his shoulder in such an unusual way it takes a five-strong team to pull it into place. Alan, who is in his sixties and had one leg amputated in 2009, comes in with the other dangerously swollen following a holiday in Goa, and 83-year-old James has to be persuaded by senior sister AnnMarie to accept treatment for a cyst on his knee

A man with an injured finger following a night of clubbing contemplates his upcoming gender reassignment therapy, which will include taking hormones, while an 80-year-old with terminal cancer arrives after collapsing at her home

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Europes biggest brewery - Heineken in the Netherlands - on a mission to explain the difference between lager and ale. His journey takes a surprising twist when he comes face to face with the mother of all lagers. Kate Quilton sets Matt Tebbutt the challenge of making crumpets, and finds out how they get filled with unique holes at the Warburtons factory in Enfield, north London. Matt also heads for snowy Canada to discover why maple syrup is so expensive, and finds out that maple syrup robberies are a big concern to the industry

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The camping connoisseurs arrive at Cefn Coed, nestled within an ancient oak forest near the Mawddach Estuary, where highlights include forest bathing and a Thai cooking class

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit the Cotswolds and Somerset, where finds include a set of milk churns and an old munitions shell

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: In La Zenia, Alicante, Jean Johansson helps house-hunters Alan and Julie to find the perfect holiday home. Theyre looking for a three-bed villa or town house close to amenities for their budget of £140,000. Jean shows them five superb properties, but will any of them be the right place for Alan and Julie?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host in Edinburgh is property manager and law student Sharon, who has gone for a culinary nod to her Australasian travels, complete with a didgeridoo for good measure. The guests shes gunning to impress are taxi driver Andrew, management consultant Anthony, museum worker Carmen and geography teacher Ashley

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Management consultant Anthony provides an evening of fine food, the arrival of a new guest shocks the diners and Ashley gets rather competitive over a game of table-top croquet

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Library worker Carmen hosts the third evening in Edinburgh, serving up posh versions of her childhood favourites, so she asks her guests to come dressed as their 10-year-old selves. At the table, writer Gerald is less than impressed by management consultant Anthony and law student Sharons constant bickering, while teacher Ashleigh confronts Sharon over her old-fashioned views

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Edinburgh, American-born Gerald takes his guests Stateside with a menu of diner classics, but he soon finds himself on the back foot when finicky foodie Anthony gives his grub a grilling. Later theres Presidential name dropping, dubious doppelgangers and as the evening draws to a close, preening and pouting when the diners take part in a bizarre beauty pageant

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Edinburgh, geography teacher Ashley hopes her magical evening of enchanted food will send her to the top of the leaderboard to secure the £1,000 prize. The soiree begins well with American Gerald, whose fear of mushed up peas is banished over the pea soup starter and even Anthony has his tastes changed when it comes to curried scallops. Fussy-eater Sharon, however has a few harsh words to share over her main of wild-caught trout, while museum worker Carmen enjoys a front row seat to the continued bickering between Anthony and Sharon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to the Cliff House Hotel in Southbourne, where owner Rich Norris and chef Matt Head hope that their swish decor and top-notch breakfast will give them the edge. Guests Hannah and Ibrahim are blown away by their bathroom, but Paul and Dave end up feeling a little exposed. At dinner, a revelation about Ibrahims high-flying corporate career raises eyebrows, while the hosts culinary credentials raise eggs-pectations for the morning. At breakfast, Stuart and Fiona discover some fishy goings-on, before Hannahs verdict on the food leaves Rich and Matt feeling scrambled

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second venue of the week is the Hotel Barge Waternimf in Ely, Cambridgeshire, which has an open plan kitchen leaving the hosts exposed to everything the guests have to say

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Montpellier Hotel in Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where Hannah Henewaa and Ibrahim Sani hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Paul Wright and Dave McDermott welcome their rivals to the Coach and Horses in West Sussex, where one guest has a bad nights sleep and the fry-up falls flat for some of the others

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment Day sees the BandBers take each other to task on everything from insufficient slumber to hopeless hosting, before the payments are revealed and we find out whos won

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Eastbourne on a quest to secretly restore a 1962 Mini Cooper S. This particular model belongs to farmer Keith, who has raced and rallied cars for most of his life, until he was struck down with serious heart problems. Although his racing days are over, Keith cant let go of his beloved Mini Cooper, even though it has been rotting away in a shed for decades

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw revisit vehicles from the first four series to see how life has changed for the owners. Featuring an appearance by Roger Moore

programme.title: The Dog House Australia
programme.desc: The Prestia family must decide whether to take on a bonded pair of dogs and retired nuns Margaret and Antoinette hope to find their dog companion. Plus, can Justin the magician take on large puppy Oreos tricks?

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend restore a 2002 Suzuki Jimny. This cherished 4x4, filled with memories, was gifted to retired NHS nurse Joanna by her late father

programme.title: Saving Country Houses with Penelope Keith
programme.desc: In Northamptonshire, Nova Guest has decided to get to grips with Ashby Manors expansive parkland, and restore the views to the original vision of architect Edwin Lutyens. At Mapperton House, valuable tapestries are brought out for conservation. The staff at Chillingham Castle, Northumberland, explain the challenges of working for lifelong collector Sir Humphry Wakefield, who keeps buying new items and antiques

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 21-year-old woman on her first trip to London from Northern Ireland appears to be experiencing kidney failure, and medics fear she could be at risk of a cardiac arrest. A man in a critical condition after falling 20ft from a balcony is brought in by helicopter, and a 34-year-old who fell off her platform shoes while dancing is treated for a deep gash on her forehead

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Ninety-three-year-old Ernest is brought in with life-threatening injuries after being knocked down by a hit-and-run driver just outside the hospital. He goes into cardiac arrest, and a consultant must keep his heart going manually in a bid to save his life. Meanwhile, an 83-year-olds finger is mangled by a machine at his metal-working business, and a 51-year-old man with learning difficulties is treated after collapsing, prompting his elderly mother to wonder how he will eventually cope after her death

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 21-year-old woman on her first trip to London from Northern Ireland appears to be experiencing kidney failure, and medics fear she could be at risk of a cardiac arrest. A man in a critical condition after falling 20ft from a balcony is brought in by helicopter, and a 34-year-old who fell off her platform shoes while dancing is treated for a deep gash on her forehead

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Ninety-three-year-old Ernest is brought in with life-threatening injuries after being knocked down by a hit-and-run driver just outside the hospital. He goes into cardiac arrest, and a consultant must keep his heart going manually in a bid to save his life. Meanwhile, an 83-year-olds finger is mangled by a machine at his metal-working business, and a 51-year-old man with learning difficulties is treated after collapsing, prompting his elderly mother to wonder how he will eventually cope after her death

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics find a woman whos fallen, is on blood thinners and may have a bleed on the brain. A man is finding it hard to communicate and his face is drooped on the right-hand side; all tell-tale signs of a possible stroke. The family of a baby with a complex medical history ring for help when his heart begins beating extremely fast

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The stories of people who changed the course of their lives by taking huge risks, including a consultant who swapped an army career for the frontline of emergency medicine. Two motorcyclists are brought in after high speed impacts, while a retired coal miner experiences major chest pains

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: At Queens Medical Centre in Nottingham, one of the busiest AandE departments in Europe, 77-year-old Keith is treated for a cardiac arrest and Joanna has a spinal fracture after falling from her horse. In the Urgent Treatment Unit, 79-year-old John is being patched up after being run over by a car at his local market

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of the teams favourite investigations, including Kate Quiltons visit to McVities in Carlisle to find out why so many biscuits have little holes all over them. Jimmy Doherty heads to Portugal to see how corks are made for wine bottles, while Matt Tebbutt is in Italy to learn why mascarpone is more expensive than other soft cheeses

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: This weeks camping connoisseurs go in search of the perfect pitch in north Wales. Their first stop is the coastal campsite of Porth Iago on the Llyn Peninsula

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole gives Simon OBrien a motorcycling masterclass in Wiltshire as the duo seek more items to fix up and sell on to make profits for their owners

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Married couple Mo and Anthony seek a holiday apartment with a sea view in Torremolinos on Spains Costa del Sol. Laura Hamilton is tasked with showing them the best of what their £130,000 budget can get them. However, whether any of the five properties the property expert has up her sleeve will result in an offer remains to be seen

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Jen is the first person to stage for a week of dinner parties in Glasgow, serving up a pink-themed evening with a straightforward menu to impress her guests. She needs to pull out all the stops to impress partnerships manager Dorine, food vlogger Darren, psychic medium Robert and nail technician Kasia of her virues as host

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Psychic medium Robert is the second to host in and around Glasgow, hoping to wow his guests with a glimpse into their futures and a menu replicating his wedding meal in Turkey

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Glasgow food vlogger Darren serves up a menu his guests are unlikely to forget in a hurry as he is deep frying everything on it, including lasagne, lettuce and chocolate bars

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Next to host in and around the Glasgow area is 28-year-old partnerships manager Dorine, who opts for a bit of sparkle with glitter and gold, and a French-inspired menu sprinkled with flavours of her childhood. However, tensions between Darren and Kasia remain

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Last to host in and around the Glasgow area is Polish nail technician Kasia, who hopes to scoop the £1,000 prize with an evening of traditional Polish food. It proves an entertaining night with a lively game of Polish Horseshoes, boys versus girls knocking bottles over with frisbees and confessions of phobias around the table leaving everyone open-mouthed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off in rural Co Durham at Bowlees Farm Holiday Cottages, where married couple Tony and Viv Smith hope that their tranquil setting, private hot tubs and indoor swimming pool will impress their visitors. Their guests are bowled over by the views and the facilities, but friends Jane and Rachel find fault with the cleanliness in the kitchen. At dinner, its revealed that breakfast will be provided by the local farm shop, the only facility that Tony and Viv dont seem to have. Despite the clean plates, married couple Amanda and Howard make their feelings clear on the lack of options

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the Piebald Inn in Hunmanby, North Yorkshire, where the lack of a thermostat and a divisive design idea irritate some of the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Stoneleigh House Hotel, a boutique townhouse in the heart of Buckingham run by Jane Parslow and Rachel Rawding. At dinner, the guests are filled in about the Piebalds main attraction, but Jane and Viv dont see pie to pie about a design idea. The group members have a mixed nights sleep, but they all agree about the quality of the breakfast - which is topped off by a surprise from John

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final venue is glamping site Evenlode Grounds in the Cotswolds, owned by Howard and Amanda Wooliams, who impress their guests by revealing it is also a working farm

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners meet for the last time to find out what theyve been paid and to settle some scores. First up to fire back on their feedback are Tony and Viv, followed by John and Lesley, who are both desperate to know why Jane and Rachel were the only two who wouldnt return to their BandBs

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: The BMW 2002 Turbo was the first European production car to have a turbo-charged engine. It also happens to be on Tim Shaws all-time favourite list. The vehicle in question belongs to 79-year-old boy racer Raymond from the West Midlands, who has owned the car, which was built in 1974, for over 30 years. But Ray now has health problems and his beloved car has ended up rusting on his drive. When Tim and Fuzz Townshend get the vehicle to the workshop, they discover that its going to be even more work than they were expecting

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a Welsh motoring enthusiasts beloved Vauxhall Astra back to its former glory

programme.title: The Dog House Australia
programme.desc: Three-year-old Ollie and his family search for a forever friend. Can Frankie the pup help Simon overcome a childhood dog attack trauma? Jaran has the chance to become Liz and Scotts first dog

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin meets Mike and Sarah, who bought a remote smallholding in the Derbyshire Dales, somewhere to rear animals and provide a home for themselves and their elderly parents. Kevin follows the couple as they set out to build a 21st-century version of a historic Derbyshire longhouse. Enormous, corrugated and with a highly complex roof shape, it was never going to be an easy build. But soon they face delays and their schedule begins to slide. However, that is nothing compared with the problems of a more personal nature that hit the project

programme.title: Our Farm Next Door: Amanda, Clive and Kids
programme.desc: As Christmas looms, Amanda and family face re-roofing Anty Johns during a fearsome Dales winter, which follows a wet and stormy autumn that has left the project months behind schedule. Builder Richard and roofer Steven must do their best to complete the arduous and technical challenge of positioning dozens of stone slabs, while trying to stay dry and warm

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A builder falls at work and dislocates his shoulder in such an unusual way it takes a five-strong team to pull it into place. Alan, who is in his sixties and had one leg amputated in 2009, comes in with the other dangerously swollen following a holiday in Goa, and 83-year-old James has to be persuaded by senior sister AnnMarie to accept treatment for a cyst on his knee

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old Josh is flown in with a possible penile fracture resulting from a canal barge accident, and while his parents make jokes to try to lighten the atmosphere, the injury could have serious repercussions for his future. programme.title: 999: On the Front Line

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Saving Giraffes: The Long Journey Home
programme.desc: Endangered animals become a symbol; of hope to a community torn apart by conflict in a National Geographic film directed by Tyler Schiffman. The Eastern Rift Valley in Africa is the ancestral home of the Rothchilds giraffe but these majestic creatures were wiped out in the region 70 years ago. Save Giraffes Now, The Ruko Conservancy, Northern Rangelands Trust and the Kenya Wildlife Service collaborate to rescue nine giraffes from a sinking island in Lake Baringo and relocate the animals to a 44,000 acre wildlife conservancy. Two tribes, who were previously at war, come together to save the giraffes and this inspirational story is captured in Schiffmans film

programme.title: Edwardian Britain in Colour
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary in which social historians examine surviving film footage of Britain in the early years of the 20th century. The first episode features footage of Queen Victorias state funeral in 1901 and a bicycle parade celebrating the coronation of the new king. The programme also examines life for people employed in the mining and cotton industries, as well as how an increase in leisure time led to the the popularity of seaside resorts soaring, with Blackpool leading the way

programme.title: Victorian Britain on Film
programme.desc: Documentary offering a unique window into a bygone era when a thrilling new invention, the motion picture camera, first captured a nation on film. Most of these films have been transformed by colourising them for the first time. They offer a rare portrait of a powerful and prosperous nation - and Empire - on the cusp of great change

programme.title: Besieged Fortresses, Legendary Battles
programme.sub-title: The Daunting Fortress of Richard the Lionheart
programme.desc: How fortresses redefined the art of warfare, illustrated with the aid of CGI, dramatised scenes and contributions by key experts. The first programme details the daunting fortress of Richard the Lionheart - a story of an amazing feat of medieval military construction

programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary in which social historians examine surviving film footage of Britain in the early years of the 20th century. The first episode features footage of Queen Victorias state funeral in 1901 and a bicycle parade celebrating the coronation of the new king. The programme also examines life for people employed in the mining and cotton industries, as well as how an increase in leisure time led to the the popularity of seaside resorts soaring, with Blackpool leading the way

programme.title: Hidden Letters
programme.desc: Following two millennial Chinese women connected by their fascination with the secret language of Nushu, a centuries-old hidden language that bonded generations of Chinese women in a clandestine support system of sisterhood, hope and survival. Influenced by Nushu?s legacy of female solidarity, the two women struggle to find balance as they forge their own paths in a patriarchal culture

programme.title: Victorian Britain on Film
programme.desc: Documentary offering a unique window into a bygone era when a thrilling new invention, the motion picture camera, first captured a nation on film. Most of these films have been transformed by colourising them for the first time. They offer a rare portrait of a powerful and prosperous nation - and Empire - on the cusp of great change

programme.title: The Bush Years: Family, Duty, Power
programme.desc: An exploration of the US presidential familys internal dynamics, including the influential matriarchs, sibling ambitions and competitive spirit that drove them to power. After returning from war, George HW Bush was reunited with his sweetheart Barbara. They married and had their first child - another future president

programme.title: Edwardian Britain in Colour
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary in which social historians examine surviving film footage of Britain in the early years of the 20th century. The first episode features footage of Queen Victorias state funeral in 1901 and a bicycle parade celebrating the coronation of the new king. The programme also examines life for people employed in the mining and cotton industries, as well as how an increase in leisure time led to the the popularity of seaside resorts soaring, with Blackpool leading the way

programme.title: Victorian Britain on Film
programme.desc: Documentary offering a unique window into a bygone era when a thrilling new invention, the motion picture camera, first captured a nation on film. Most of these films have been transformed by colourising them for the first time. They offer a rare portrait of a powerful and prosperous nation - and Empire - on the cusp of great change

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Northern Ireland
programme.sub-title: It Wasnt Like a Movie Anymore
programme.desc: Stories of the Troubles from people on all sides of the conflict, beginning by looking at how the situation in Northern Ireland in the late-1960s deteriorated from a time of relative peace. As the civil rights movement gained pace, demanding equal rights for Catholics, the Provisional IRA came to the fore, causing the British Army to be deployed. With the situation compounded by tactical mistakes, soldiers quickly found themselves caught up in violent clashes - leading to 13 unarmed Catholics being shot dead by British paratroopers in Derry on what becomes known as Bloody Sunday

programme.title: Weathered: Earths Extremes
programme.sub-title: Are We at the Tipping Point?
programme.desc: Maiya May journeys across the United States to explore climate tipping points. Discovering how critical climate systems are for the planet?s stability, and learning the science behind these changes

programme.title: The Bush Years: Family, Duty, Power
programme.desc: An exploration of the US presidential familys internal dynamics, including the influential matriarchs, sibling ambitions and competitive spirit that drove them to power. After returning from war, George HW Bush was reunited with his sweetheart Barbara. They married and had their first child - another future president

programme.title: The Invention of Surgery
programme.sub-title: The Secret of the Human Body
programme.desc: Exploring the history of surgery from its rudimentary conception centuries ago up to the modern specialist procedures that take place today. Beginning with a look at the risks people took to understand the human anatomy, how they managed to control human blood flow and how French king Louis XIV helped surgery become a recognised science. Part one of two

programme.desc: How, blinded by loyalty to his president and ignoring the advice of his family, George HW Bush was betrayed by Richard Nixon

programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part documentary in which social historians examine surviving footage of Britain in the early years of the 20th century. programme.title: Armstrong
programme.desc: Harrison Ford tells the life story of Neil Armstrong, from his childhood in rural Ohio, through aerial combat in Korea, to his first steps on the moon and the unwanted celebrity status that came along after. Including interviews with those who knew the astronaut and aviator best

programme.title: Besieged Fortresses, Legendary Battles
programme.sub-title: The Siege of LA Rochelle, the Fall of the Protestant Bastion
programme.desc: La Rochelle was the largest Protestant base in France and for Catholic king Louis XIII was the last stronghold needing to be abolished before he could reign fully over the country. However, conquering La Rochelle proved more difficult than imagined

programme.title: Edwardian Britain in Colour

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Northern Ireland
programme.sub-title: Do Paramilitaries Lie Awake at Night?
programme.desc: A look at the growth of paramilitary organisations in the early 1970s, with killings, explosions, intimidation and street disorder becoming widespread - with devastating and often lasting effects. Coercive control, intimidation, beatings and executions were part of how these groups operated in their different communities, a world in which people could be disappeared, never to be found for decades afterwards

programme.title: The Bush Years: Family, Duty, Power
programme.desc: How, blinded by loyalty to his president and ignoring the advice of his family, George HW Bush was betrayed by Richard Nixon

programme.title: The Invention of Surgery
programme.desc: Exploring the early experiments with ether and chloroform and what changed dramatically as a result of their use. Plus, how surgeons looked for ways to prevent wound infections and what technique finally brought them success. Part two of two

programme.desc: Jay Blades and his team of experts meet Liz from Dorset who brings in a crumbling cast-iron gate built by her brother as a gift for her and her late husband. programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: First into the Repair Shop is Alison who brings with her an 1890s ventriloquists puppet called George. The puppet helped Alison grow in confidence and gave her an outlet for her undiagnosed dyslexia as a child but hes now broken both inside and out. Organ-restorer David Burville brings Kirsten Ramsay and the teddy bear ladies to help restore George to his former glory. Lewys from Wales brings in a brass cornet dating back to 1897 that has passed through generations of his family. What once filled his home with music is now completely unplayable, instrument expert Pete Woods gets to work. programme.title: How Do They Do It?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?

programme.desc: Mike Brewer and Marc Priestley help farmer Tom trade up an old Jaguar and a tractor to a DeLorean, but with a £30,000 price tag it wont be an easy task

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Henry Cole and motorcycle restorer Sam Lovegrove head to Herefordshire, where they discover sheds full of engines, autogyros and weird and wonderful vehicles of all kinds, before coming away with a British classic

programme.title: Shed and Buried

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Phil Serrell and Catherine Southon - Day 4
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Phil Serrell embark on the fourth leg of their trip travelling through the Home Counties of Buckinghamshire and Hertfordshire, as they head towards their penultimate auction. Catherine shells out for not one but two vintage chairs, a Murano glass bead necklace, an ebonised elephant head page turner, a police helmet and a cheese mould. Phil splashes serious cash on some Murano of his own, a stylish library chair, horses sculpted in leather, an elegant mirror and a set of antique scales

programme.desc: When a father of five is found dead on his kitchen floor, Det. James Colley must untangle a web of twisted relationships and bizarre fantasies to uncover the mystery

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the story of how Border Force cutters the HMC Seeker and HMC Searcher tracked a fishing vessel suspected of carrying a shipment of cocaine. Plus, drunken driver Thomas Burney, who drove into and killed 19-year-old pedestrian Bryony Hollands in 2015

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: When an innocent woman in a quiet town was shot in broad daylight, detectives immediately acted to find her killer, referencing video surveillance as possible evidence

programme.title: Body Cam
programme.desc: In Oklahoma, a drug suspect sparks a perilous chase. In Michigan, a gunman fires on police, and in Missouri, a sting unravels when the suspect bolts

programme.title: Road Rage
programme.desc: A woman bites another driver. A young woman takes evasive manoeuvres to survive a road-raging driver. Motorists fight in a drive-thru

programme.title: Road Rage
programme.desc: Good Samaritans rescue road rage victims. Californians negotiate dangerous roadways. A man defends his child from a gun-toting menace

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the story of a woman whose ex-husband killed their sons during an unsupervised visit, as well as the case of a elderly womans carer who was at the centre of a web of deceit and fraudulent activity

programme.title: Britains Deadliest Kids
programme.desc: The case of James Fairweather, who terrorised a local community with random deadly attacks that left two people dead in May 2015. He was planning to murder a third when apprehended. What made him carry out such brutal murders?

programme.title: Britains Deadliest Kids
programme.desc: An investigation into teen lovers Kim Edwards and Lucas Markham, who were convicted of murdering Kims mother Elizabeth and younger sister Katie in April 2016

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In 2014, a student from China is found murdered in an Iowa college town. Surveillance footage points to a planned execution and it opens an international investigation

programme.desc: Singer-songwriter KT Tunstall always knew she was adopted and in 1998 was reunited with her birth mother, who told her that her father never wanted to give her up - a message that has inspired her to find out what happened to him. Brother and sister Simon Munro and Carole Killick grew up apart after their mother left when they were young in the 1960s, but the siblings were reunited more than 20 years ago and are now searching for their mother. Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow Kathleen Fraser Jacksons search for her mother, with the 62-year-old having been trying to track down the woman who gave her up for adoption for more than 40 years. In a last-ditch attempt to help Kathleen find her mum, the Long Lost Family team samples Kathleens DNA and adds it to an online database, which triggers an epic journey spanning continents and cultures, and unlocking more secrets - and family members - than she could ever have dreamed of

programme.title: Celebrity Yorkshire Auction House

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Alice Jones was brought up by her father, but on a visit to her mother more than 50 years ago she met her younger brother called Sam and has always hoped that one day she would see him again. Mark Ratcliffe grew up in Lancashire and at the age of 12 his parents told him he was adopted and the revelation devastated him. Ever since, he cannot shake the question of how a mother could give up her child and is now looking for answers

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Martin Smith grew up in Rochdale and had a happy childhood, but struggled with intense feelings of rejection when he discovered he was adopted and only now feels ready to face his fears and find his birth mother. Anne Jordan always knew she was adopted and has been searching for her birth mother for over 30 years, but she will not set a date for her wedding to fiance Chris until she has found her

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: JJ Chalmers saves some horse tack, a wicker chair and an old chest from the recycling centre, which he and crafters Rosanna Gethin and Lucianne Hinch try to reinvent for sale

programme.desc: A hunt for fresh stock takes Drew Pritchard to the Cotswolds, where he finds a giant stash of Indian and Afghan treasures which have come directly from the subcontinent

programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott travel around Northamptonshire, where items include an art deco trinket box, a 1905 kaleidoscope, a BandB sign and an early Japanese satsuma pot

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Sue Perkins discovers more about her orphaned grandfather and a great grandfather interned as an enemy alien during the First World War. When the comedian and broadcaster delves into the German branch of her family who were living in Eastern Europe through the Second World War, she uncovers a harrowing tale of refugees fleeing back and forth across borders between Nazi and Soviet control

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit more of the most extraordinary searches featured previously on the show to find out how lives have been transformed, beginning with the stories of people desperate to find their birth parents. Among those featured are sisters Anne Jordan and Flo Banfield, who first met in 2019 and have spent the last two years on a mission to reconnect with the maternal side of their family
